title: Detlef van Vuuren named climate scientist most cited by the IPCC
content: Professor Detlef van Vuuren has been ranked as the sixth most cited climate scientist in the world in a new analysis by Carbon Brief. He is also the climate scientist most cited by the Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change (IPCC).

title: ERC Advanced Grants for three researchers from Utrecht
content: Joris Eggenhuisen, Frank Biermann and Frank van Tubergen have been awarded an ERC Advanced Grant. This will enable them to develop projects that could lead to breakthroughs.
snippet: Joris Eggenhuisen, Frank Biermann and Frank van Tubergen have been awarded an ERC Advanced Grant. This will enable them to develop projects that could lead to breakthroughs.

title: Delta Climate Center investigates the natural history of Zeeland’s landscapes
content: The aim is to gather new scientific insights into the history of the Zeeland landscape’s formation.
snippet: The aim is to gather new scientific insights into the history of the Zeeland landscape’s formation.

title: In NanoMedNL, patients, physicians, companies, and regulators are involved in nanomedicine development from the start
content: Recently, the NanoMedNL project was officially launched, bringing together 19 partners to collaborate on a new generation of nanomedicines.
snippet: Recently, the NanoMedNL project was officially launched, bringing together 19 partners to collaborate on a new generation of nanomedicines.

title: SAVE THE DATE for our webinar on social innovation and prefiguration around the world | 10.06.2026
content: Social innovation webinar exploring pluriversal perspectives and prefigurative movements worldwide on 10 June 2026; registration now open.
snippet: Social innovation webinar exploring pluriversal perspectives and prefigurative movements worldwide on 10 June 2026; registration now open.

title: Rural Dutch households hit hardest by high energy prices
content: New research by Utrecht University and TNO, published today in the economics policy journal ESB, shows that households in rural regions spend a significantly larger share of their disposable income on car fuel, electricity and home heating.
snippet: New research by Utrecht University and TNO, published today in the economics policy journal ESB, shows that households in rural regions spend a significantly larger share of their disposable income on

title: Open Call: Contributing Authors for the International Panel on Earth System Governance (IPESG)
content: The International Panel on Earth System Governance is seeking Contributing Authors for its first global assessment of research on earth system governance.
snippet: The International Panel on Earth System Governance is seeking Contributing Authors for its first global assessment of research on earth system governance.

title: Prestigious grant for sustainability education combining art, science and action
content: The aim of the recipients of the Comenius Leadership Fellow grant is to set up an educational programme that trains students who can truly drive the sustainability transition.
snippet: The aim of the recipients of the Comenius Leadership Fellow grant is to set up an educational programme that trains students who can truly drive the sustainability transition.

title: Over 11,000 children are contributing to scientific research
content: During the so-called Schoolyard Safari, 11,254 pupils in Years 6 to 8 collected all sorts of data for genuine scientific research about biodiversity in a single day.
snippet: During the so-called Schoolyard Safari, 11,254 pupils in Years 6 to 8 collected all sorts of data for genuine scientific research about biodiversity in a single day.

title: How sustainable is education at Utrecht University? Join the conversation on 23 June
content: Over the past year, researcher Tim Stevens explored the integration of sustainability across all degree programmes through a university-wide study.
snippet: Over the past year, researcher Tim Stevens explored the integration of sustainability across all degree programmes through a university-wide study.

title: From physicist to solar pioneer
content: As he retires, Prof. Wilfried Van Sark reflects on a career driven by curiosity, interdisciplinarity, and a belief in the power of students as “change agents”.
snippet: As he retires, Prof. Wilfried Van Sark reflects on a career driven by curiosity, interdisciplinarity, and a belief in the power of students as “change agents”.

title: New research offers cities four pathways to urban circularity
content: Researchers identiy four distinct approaches to circularity cities take that could help policymakers learn from peers facing similar challenges and opportunities.
snippet: Researchers identiy four distinct approaches to circularity cities take that could help policymakers learn from peers facing similar challenges and opportunities.

title: New global emission scenarios: changes in the highest and lowest scenarios
content: A new set of global emissions scenarios that will form the basis for much climate research in the coming years.
snippet: A new set of global emissions scenarios that will form the basis for much climate research in the coming years.

title: NWO grant for research on education for societal change
content: ENLITE will receive 220.000 euros to continue their research on educating agents of change.
snippet: ENLITE will receive 220.000 euros to continue their research on educating agents of change.

title: From Sri Lanka to Utrecht University
content: Global Sustainability Science Alumnus Droovi de Zilva’s journey reflects how interdisciplinary education, hands-on experiences and curiosity can shape a meaningful career in sustainability.
snippet: Global Sustainability Science Alumnus Droovi de Zilva’s journey reflects how interdisciplinary education, hands-on experiences and curiosity can shape a meaningful career in sustainability.

title: Never running out – Research into reusable hygiene products receives NWO funding
content: Why are reusable alternatives not widely used, and what measures could encourage consumers to switch to reusable options?
snippet: Why are reusable alternatives not widely used, and what measures could encourage consumers to switch to reusable options?

title: Climate change and deforestation interact to cause cascading impacts across the Amazon
content: Study providing the most detailed quantification yet of how warming and deforestation simultaneously impact the stability of the Amazon.
snippet: Study providing the most detailed quantification yet of how warming and deforestation simultaneously impact the stability of the Amazon.

title: Four Geosciences colleagues contributing to IPCC reports
content: The International Panel on Climate Change (IPCC) is one of the world’s most influential institutions in its field. On behalf of the UN, it is tasked with assessing the science related to climate change, and ‘provide governments at all levels with scientific information that they can use to develop climate policies.

title: Colonial legacy still drives loss of species and languages worldwide
content: Human activities have always shaped both biological and cultural diversity. A new study has investigated the link between the threat to species and that to languages.
snippet: Human activities have always shaped both biological and cultural diversity. A new study has investigated the link between the threat to species and that to languages.

title: Looking back with Prof. Peter Driessen: “I enjoyed every day”
content: Ahead of his Valedictory Lecture on 9 April, Prof. Peter Driessen reflects on a career shaped by curiosity, real world impact, and a lasting enjoyment of academic life.
snippet: Ahead of his Valedictory Lecture on 9 April, Prof. Peter Driessen reflects on a career shaped by curiosity, real world impact, and a lasting enjoyment of academic life.
